scenario: Rails work session - audit the query Relation contract before a rework

map_quality.rationale: The answer names Rails as a gem monorepo, places ActiveRecord::Relation at relation.rb:5, lists every module in the relation/ subdir by file, gives the creation path (core.rb:442 → Relation.create), user exposure (scoping/named.rb:22, querying.rb:24), and the association subclasses — an agent can pick an entry point immediately. Minor gap: the query compilation/execution side (arel, where_clause compilation) is not mentioned.

specificity.rationale: Every cited piece has a concrete file path and line number (relation.rb:5, core.rb:442, scoping/named.rb:22, querying.rb:24); all relation/ submodules are named individually rather than summarised as 'the query modules'.

justification.rationale: The answer names the pieces well but the only WHY offered is 'lazy query value … accumulates a query as it is chained'; it does not explain why execution is deferred until enumeration, why the query interface is decomposed into mixed-in modules, or why chaining produces a new relation rather than mutating in place.

uncertainty.rationale: No uncertainty is flagged anywhere; the answer presents all paths with equal confidence and does not signal what it has not opened (e.g. the execution/SQL compilation layer, the merger internals), leaving the agent with no flag to verify before acting.

relationship.rationale: The answer states modules are 'mixed into' Relation, that it is 'Created from core.rb:442 via Relation.create', 'surfaced to users through scoping/named.rb:22 (all)', and that association subclasses extend it — these are real connecting relationships, not just a file list. The spawn-a-new-relation-on-chaining relationship is absent, and the delegation module's routing path to the model class is not stated.

steps.step: Map the Relation contract and what it is built on

map_quality.rationale: All seven include'd modules are named with the exact include line (relation.rb:69-70), each has a file:line and a role phrase, and the 'What a teammate must know' section maps the immutable-by-spawn invariant plus the full state structure (@values, MULTI/SINGLE/CLAUSE_METHODS at :54-66, memoized @arel/@records reset via initialize_copy). Value-object satellites (merger, where_clause, from_clause) are also covered, giving the agent a nearly complete preservation checklist.

specificity.rationale: Concrete file:line citations are dense throughout — relation/query_methods.rb:1673/1828/1692, relation/finder_methods.rb:201/357/458, relation/spawn_methods.rb:9/33/43, relation/delegation.rb:101-106/118 — rather than vague references to 'the query modules'; the only slight gap is that relation/batches.rb and explain.rb carry no line numbers.

justification.rationale: The immutable-by-spawn design is explicitly justified ('chaining never mutates; spawn.merge! clones then mutates the copy') and the state-in-@values architecture is explained, but the answer doesn't explain WHY the interface is split into focused modules (separation of concerns, mix-in composability) or WHY delegation is needed (to route unresolved calls to model-class scopes), which are the architectural reasons most relevant to a safe change.

uncertainty.rationale: The answer presents every claim with full confidence and flags nothing it couldn't verify — no 'didn't open this method', no 'delegation internals unconfirmed', no note that Explain or Batches were not fully inspected — so the agent has no signal about where to double-check before acting.

relationship.rationale: The include line is shown verbatim so the binding mechanism is explicit, SpawnMethods' role as the cloning engine for chaining is stated in the summary section, and Delegation's routing to the model class is named with a line reference; however, the path by which QueryMethods#build_arel feeds the final SQL compilation and how Delegation#method_missing reaches model-class named scopes is described as endpoint names rather than a traversal route.

steps.step: Trace how a query is built up and executed

map_quality.rationale: The answer traces the full path in numbered order with file:line at every waypoint: base relation at core.rb:442, default scope at named.rb:22/45, spawn at spawn_methods.rb:9, merge at spawn_methods.rb:33/merger.rb:6, SQL build at query_methods.rb:1673/1828, and execution through relation.rb:1212→1436→1460→querying.rb:67/51→database_statements.rb:802.
